questions.question: A painter uses $5$ different brush sizes. The sizes are $10$, $15$, $20$, $25$, and $30$ mm. What is the $mean$ brush size?
A music band has $7$ songs with the following lengths in minutes: $3$, $4$, $5$, $6$, $7$, $8$, $9$. What is the $median$ song length?
A sculptor creates $9$ statues with the following heights in cm: $150$, $160$, $170$, $170$, $170$, $180$, $190$, $200$, $210$. What is the $mode$ height?

Two art galleries have the following number of paintings: Gallery A has $15$, $20$, $25$, $30$, $35$ paintings and Gallery B has $10$, $15$, $20$, $25$, $30$ paintings. Which gallery has a higher $mean$ number of paintings?
A musician practices for $4$, $5$, $6$, $7$, $8$ hours a day. What is the $range$ of practice hours?
A painter has $3$ canvases with the following areas in square meters: $10$, $15$, $20$. What is the $median$ area?
A composer writes $5$ songs with the following time signatures: $3/4$, $4/4$, $4/4$, $5/4$, $6/4$. What is the $mode$ time signature?

Two music albums have the following number of tracks: Album A has $10$, $12$, $15$, $18$, $20$ tracks and Album B has $8$, $10$, $12$, $15$, $18$ tracks. Which album has a higher $median$ number of tracks?

A dance company has $6$ performances with the following audience sizes: $200$, $250$, $300$, $350$, $400$, $450$. Calculate the $mean$, $median$, and $mode$ audience sizes. Show all work and explain your answers.

A painter has $8$ paintings with the following prices in dollars: $500$, $600$, $700$, $800$, $900$, $1000$, $1100$, $1200$. Calculate the $mean$, $median$, and $range$ of the prices. Explain how these measures of central tendency help describe the data set.

teacher_rules: Ensure students use proper mathematical notation and show work for all calculations.
Emphasize the importance of interpreting the results in context.
Encourage students to compare and contrast the different measures of central tendency.
